subject: History
overall_learning_objectives: Explain the background of the Revolt of 1857
Identify the political, economic and military causes of the revolt
Describe the roles of major leaders in different regions
Understand how the revolt spread across North and Central India
Explain how the British suppressed the revolt
Analyse the administrative changes introduced after 1857

lesson_plan.title: Background of the Revolt of 1857
topics_covered: Conditions of Indian soldiers and rulers
student_activities: Discuss reasons why people oppose rulers
List factors that created dissatisfaction against British rule

lesson_plan.title: Causes of the Revolt
student_activities: Write the immediate cause of the revolt
Create a list of political and economic causes

lesson_plan.title: Leaders of the Revolt
student_activities: Match leaders with the regions they led
Write one contribution of each leader

lesson_plan.title: Spread of the Revolt
topics_covered: Spread to Delhi, Kanpur, Lucknow and Jhansi
Participation of soldiers, rulers and peasants
student_activities: Mark important centres of the revolt on a map
Discuss why the revolt spread quickly

lesson_plan.title: Suppression of the Revolt
student_activities: Write two reasons why the revolt failed
Discuss difficulties faced by the rebels

lesson_plan.title: Aftermath of the Revolt and Revision
topics_covered: End of East India Company rule
Beginning of direct British Crown rule
Reorganization of the army and administration
student_activities: Write one administrative change after the revolt

teacher_tip: Encourage students to connect different causes of the revolt so they understand it as a combination of political, economic and military factors.
